frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: stores predictions about future states to support semi-flexible choice

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: computes action values from scratch using a full task model

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: caches pre-computed action values for efficient but inflexible choice

core_question: Do humans use a successor-representation algorithm intermediate between model-based and model-free reward learning?
key_assumptions: action values can be decomposed into stored predictions of future states
deliberative and habitual choice reflect distinct algorithmic families
sensitivity to task changes diagnoses the underlying learning algorithm
manipulations: changes to the task's sequence of events (transition structure)
key_findings: human choice flexibly adjusted to reward changes
human choice was relatively insensitive to changes in event sequence
behavioral signature matched successor-representation predictions in two studies
interpretations: successor representation is a computational substrate for semi-flexible human choice
introduces a subtler, more cognitive notion of habit than pure model-free caching
